title: Thenthuk (Tibetan Soup)

ingredients.title: For the Broth:
items: 6 cups chicken or vegetable stock (preferably homemade)
200g chicken breast or thigh, thinly sliced
1 tbsp Shaoxing wine (optional, for authentic depth)

instruction.title: Prepare the Dough:
steps: In a mixing bowl, combine flour and salt. Gradually add water while kneading until a smooth, elastic dough forms.
Cover with a damp cloth and let rest for 20–30 minutes.

instruction.title: Prepare the Broth:
steps: Heat a wok or deep pot with a little oil. Sauté garlic and ginger until fragrant.
Add chicken slices and cook until lightly golden.
Add mushrooms, carrot, and cabbage. Stir-fry for 2–3 minutes.
Pour in stock, soy sauce, and Shaoxing wine. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 10 minutes.
Adjust seasoning with salt, white pepper, and sesame oil.

instruction.title: Make the Noodles:
steps: Roll the dough into a log about 1 inch thick. Flatten slightly.
Using your hands, pinch off small portions and stretch/pull them directly into boiling broth (traditional 'hand-pulled' style). Alternatively, roll thin and cut into strips.
Cook noodles in the simmering broth for 3–5 minutes until tender.

instruction.title: Finish & Serve:
steps: Ladle the soup with noodles and chicken into bowls.
Garnish with spring onion, cilantro, and optional chili oil or fried shallots.

chefTips: Use homemade chicken stock for deeper flavor.
Slightly undercook vegetables so they retain crunch.
Pull noodles directly into the broth for authentic texture.
A touch of sesame oil at the end elevates aroma.
Optional: Add soft-boiled egg or tofu for variation.
